# Feature: Web Expert Agent — Anti-Hallucination & Citation Guardrails

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-WEX-003
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Make the Web Expert Agent demonstrably accurate. Today's prompt asks for citations and admits uncertainty in soft language ("be honest", "cite sources") but provides no enforcement mechanism — so the LLM still occasionally invents RFC numbers, paraphrases spec text as if quoting, and answers about evolving standards without disclosing draft status.

This feature edits **only the system prompt** (inlined by FEAT-FP-WEX-001) to add concrete, enforceable guardrails:

1. **Mandatory citation format** with an `Unverified:` prefix for any claim the model cannot tie to a specific RFC §, W3C/WHATWG spec section, or MDN page.
2. **Mandatory web-fetch triggers** — explicit conditions under which the model MUST call the web fetcher rather than answer from training data.
3. **Version honesty** — when discussing evolving specs, state the spec status (RFC, IETF Draft, W3C Working Draft, etc.) and a date qualifier.
4. **Forbidden behaviors** — an explicit "do not" list (invented RFC numbers, invented headers, invented status codes, paraphrasing as quoting).
5. **Uncertainty phrasing menu** — three sanctioned ways to express uncertainty so the model has a clear pattern to follow rather than hedging in arbitrary prose.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, naming, async/await, semicolons, Vitest+RTL) are in [.spec-lite/memory.md](.spec-lite/memory.md). Only spec-specific notes below.

### Approach

- All edits land in the inlined prompt constant in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) (the constant established by FEAT-FP-WEX-001).
- **Replace the existing `### Citation Rules` subsection** (currently a soft list under `## Reference Sources`) with a hardened, numbered ruleset that includes the mandatory citation format, the `Unverified:` prefix rule, and the forbidden-behaviors list.
- **Replace the existing `## Web Fetcher Integration` section** with a stricter version that distinguishes "MUST fetch" from "MAY fetch" cases. Currently the section says "use it when... do not fetch for common knowledge"; the new version enumerates explicit MUST conditions.
- **Add a new `## Uncertainty & Version Honesty` section** between `## Web Fetcher Integration` and `## Behavioral Rules`, defining three sanctioned uncertainty phrasings and version-status disclosure rules for evolving specs.
- **Add a corresponding rule to `## Behavioral Rules`** (preserving rule numbering established in FEAT-FP-WEX-002): "**Cite or disclaim — never both vague.** Every protocol-level claim either cites a specific RFC §/spec section, or is prefixed with `Unverified:`."
- Preserve the existing tiered Reference Sources list and the established source preference order (RFC > W3C/WHATWG > MDN > web.dev > OWASP). Those are not changing.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory.

### Out of Scope

- Implementing actual web-fetcher invocation logic from inside the LLM response loop. The fetcher is already wired in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:283-313](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L283-L313); this feature only sharpens the prompt-side rules that govern *when* the model should rely on fetched context vs disclaim. (The TODO entry "Let the web expert agent invoke `webFetcher.fetch()` as an LLM tool" already tracks the deeper tool-loop work.)
- Building a citation validator that checks RFC numbers exist. That would be runtime validation; out of scope here.
- Any TypeScript code changes. Prompt-content-only feature.

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no data model changes.

## 4. Files

- `packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts` — edit the inlined prompt constant only.
- `packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts` — add tests asserting new guardrail sections and rules are present.

## 5. Dependencies

- **FEAT-FP-WEX-001** (Inline System Prompt) — must ship first so prompt edits actually reach the LLM.
- **FEAT-FP-WEX-002** (Persona & Response Structure) — recommended but not strictly required. If FEAT-FP-WEX-002 has not yet shipped, this feature still works in isolation; rule-numbering merge conflicts in `## Behavioral Rules` will need to be reconciled at integration.

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Replace `### Citation Rules` with a hardened, numbered ruleset

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In the inlined prompt in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts), replace the existing `### Citation Rules` subsection (currently inside `## Reference Sources`) with a new version that specifies:
    - **Citation format**: every protocol-level claim MUST cite using one of these exact patterns:
      - `Per RFC <n> §<section>: <claim>` (e.g., `Per RFC 9110 §9.3.1: GET requests must be safe and idempotent.`)
      - `Per <spec-name> §<section>: <claim>` for W3C/WHATWG specs.
      - `Per MDN — <page>: <claim>` for browser-API references.
    - **`Unverified:` prefix**: if the model cannot produce a precise citation, the claim MUST be prefixed `Unverified:` and followed by `— consult <canonical source URL>`.
    - **Quoting vs paraphrasing**: direct quotes from a spec MUST be in fenced blockquotes with the citation immediately above; paraphrases MUST NOT use quotation marks.
    - **Forbidden behaviors** (explicit list):
      - Inventing RFC numbers.
      - Inventing HTTP header names, status codes, or method names not in the IANA registries.
      - Asserting browser-API behavior without an MDN or spec reference.
      - Paraphrasing spec text inside quotation marks (only verbatim quotes).
      - Citing blogs, Medium, Dev.to, Stack Overflow, Twitter/X, Reddit, or LLM training-data summaries as authoritative.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none (the prompt is the documentation).
- **Verify**: build succeeds; bundled prompt contains the literal strings `Per RFC <n> §<section>:`, `Unverified:`, and the forbidden-behaviors list items.

### TASK-002: Replace `## Web Fetcher Integration` with MUST-vs-MAY fetch rules

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Replace the existing `## Web Fetcher Integration` section in the inlined prompt with a new specification:
    - **MUST fetch** (model is required to call the fetcher when):
      - The user pastes a URL.
      - The user asks for an exact quote from a spec or RFC.
      - The user invokes `/rfc <n>` (the agent already auto-fetches via [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:284-293](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L284-L293) — the prompt rule reinforces it).
      - The user invokes `/trending` (fetch HN).
      - The question concerns specs published or revised after the model's knowledge cutoff (e.g., recent IETF drafts).
      - The user asks for current draft status or "what's the latest on X".
    - **MAY fetch** (optional when):
      - The model is uncertain and the canonical source is one of the Tier 1 references.
    - **MUST NOT fetch** (skip when):
      - Common foundational knowledge confidently held in training data (basic HTTP semantics, well-known status codes).
      - The same query has already been fetched in the current conversation.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; bundled prompt contains the literal strings `MUST fetch`, `MAY fetch`, `MUST NOT fetch`.

### TASK-003: Add `## Uncertainty & Version Honesty` section

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Insert a new `## Uncertainty & Version Honesty` section in the inlined prompt, immediately after `## Web Fetcher Integration` and before `## Behavioral Rules`.
  - Section content must specify:
    - **Three sanctioned uncertainty phrasings** (model MUST pick one rather than ad-hoc hedging):
      1. `Unverified: <claim> — consult <canonical URL> to confirm.`
      2. `The spec doesn't define this; common implementations do <X> (see <source>).`
      3. `This was true as of <date / version>; check <source> for current status.`
    - **Version-status disclosure**: when discussing any of the following, the model MUST disclose status and date:
      - IETF drafts (cite `draft-<wg>-<name>-<NN>` and "as of <date>").
      - W3C Working Drafts / Candidate Recommendations (cite status level and date).
      - Living standards (e.g., HTML/Fetch — note "living standard, current as of <date>").
      - Any proposal not yet published as a final RFC.
    - **Knowledge-cutoff acknowledgment**: when a question concerns specs likely revised after the model's knowledge cutoff, the model MUST trigger a fetch (per the MUST-fetch list in TASK-002) or use uncertainty phrasing #3.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; bundled prompt contains the literal section header `## Uncertainty & Version Honesty` and all three sanctioned phrasing templates.

### TASK-004: Add a "Cite or disclaim" Behavioral Rule

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In the existing `## Behavioral Rules` section of the inlined prompt, add a new numbered rule (insert near the top, after the rules added by FEAT-FP-WEX-002 if that feature has shipped): "**Cite or disclaim — never vague.** Every protocol-level claim either cites a specific RFC §/spec section, or is prefixed with `Unverified:` per `## Uncertainty & Version Honesty`. There is no third option."
  - Add a companion rule: "**Honor MUST-fetch triggers.** Before answering, scan the user message for the MUST-fetch conditions in `## Web Fetcher Integration`. If any apply, call the fetcher first."
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; bundled prompt contains both new rule strings.

### TASK-005: Unit tests asserting guardrail sections and rules are present

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In [packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts](packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts), add a `describe('anti-hallucination & citation guardrails', ...)` block.
  - **Test 1 — Hardened Citation Rules present**: invoke `chat()`, capture `SystemMessage`, assert `.content` includes `'Per RFC'`, `'Unverified:'`, and the forbidden-behaviors phrases (`'Inventing RFC numbers'`, `'Inventing HTTP header names'`).
  - **Test 2 — MUST-fetch ruleset present**: assert `.content` includes `'MUST fetch'`, `'MAY fetch'`, `'MUST NOT fetch'`, and at least three of the MUST-fetch trigger conditions (URL paste, exact quote, `/rfc`, `/trending`).
  - **Test 3 — Uncertainty section present**: assert `.content` includes `'## Uncertainty & Version Honesty'` and all three sanctioned phrasing templates (key fragments: `'Unverified:'` with `'— consult'`, `'The spec doesn\'t define this'`, `'This was true as of'`).
  - **Test 4 — Behavioral rules present**: assert `.content` includes `'Cite or disclaim'` and `'Honor MUST-fetch triggers'`.
  - Reuse existing `makeMockLLM()` and `fetchSpy` infrastructure.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: this task *is* the test task.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena test webExpertAgent` passes; all four new tests appear green.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004.

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: N/A — prompt-content-only changes.
- **Logging**: N/A.
- **Security**: Prompt is static text shipped in the bundle; no secrets, no new attack surface. The tightened "no blogs/social media" rule reduces the chance of recommending compromised or low-quality sources.
- **Performance**: Adds ~100–150 lines to the prompt, modest per-call input-token-cost increase. The MUST-fetch rules may increase fetcher invocation rate at runtime (an *intended* effect — accuracy over speed). No hot-path latency concerns.
- **Backwards compatibility**: Per memory General §3, not required.
